differentials.name: Optic glioma
differentials.raw: Optic glioma (in 12% of patients, in 4% bilateral)

context: √  plexiform neurofibroma with vascular narrowing Origin:   vesicoprostatic (male) / urethrovaginal neural plexus (female) √  solitary hypoechoic bladder wall mass √  diffuse bladder wall thickening; mass may surround uterus, vagina, sigmoid colon √  scalloped contour of urinary bladder Cx:  hydronephrosis Ocular Manifestations in NF1 (6%) Age:  5-10 years; > 20 years of age in > 90% Age:  75% in 1st decade √  extension into optic chiasm (up to 25%), optic tracts, optic radiation √  increased intensity on T2WI if chiasm and visual pathway involved
